target: Shared-server-model architecture: ONNX replacement/augmentation for clusterable shodh instances

critical_findings.edge: Clean &str -> Vec<f32> contract. Mutex<Session> serializes inference. No batch API at trait level.
critical_findings.fractal: All 4 models follow lazy-Session+Mutex pattern. HttpEmbedder breaks pattern (the seam for shared serving).
critical_findings.hazop_critical: MORE: N instances = linear memory scaling. REVERSE: no path for cluster peer to request embeddings.

critical_findings.edge: Topic-based pub/sub for memory ops only. No embedding request channel. No leader election.
critical_findings.fractal: Memory ops are per-user isolated; embedding serving is user-agnostic. Different sharing topologies.
critical_findings.hazop_critical: PART OF: no mechanism for partial-cluster embedding routing.

critical_findings.edge: Per-user isolation at filesystem. Cluster would need distributed state or shared storage.
critical_findings.fractal: Break under clustering: concurrent writes from peers have no merge strategy (no CRDT, no vector clock).

fmea.failure_mode: Model weights version desync across cluster nodes
fmea.mitigation: Enforce model version in Zenoh service advertisement; reject mismatched dimensions
Include dimension in embedding response metadata; validate on receipt

fmea.failure_mode: Mutex contention under batch load from cluster peers
fmea.mitigation: TEI sidecar eliminates in-process Mutex; uses continuous batching

phase_2_fractal_atam.core_pattern: Monolithic edge-node: single binary, all capabilities co-located, per-user filesystem isolation
fractal_breaks: Embedding (stateless, shareable) vs Storage/Search/Graph (stateful, per-user) -- only embedding cleanly separates
Memory operations (per-user isolated) vs Embedding requests (user-agnostic) -- different sharing topologies
Circuit breaker wraps only MiniLM, not Nomic/NER -- inconsistent resilience at meso level

single_binary_vs_sidecar.sensitivity: Edge/robotics deployment strongly favors single binary; multi-instance server deployment favors sidecar
single_binary_vs_sidecar.tradeoff: TEI sidecar breaks zero-dependency story but enables model sharing; candle migration preserves single binary

network_hop_vs_memory.sensitivity: Every recall query hits embedding path; network hop adds 1-5ms
network_hop_vs_memory.tradeoff: Acceptable if TEI batching recovers more than hop costs under concurrent load

per_instance_cache_vs_shared_cache.sensitivity: moka LRU caches are per-instance; cluster has cache miss amplification
per_instance_cache_vs_shared_cache.tradeoff: TEI handles its own caching; client-side cache can still help for repeated queries

phase_2_5_adversarial.evidence: TEI adds external process dependency to zero-dependency binary. For robotics/edge (Zenoh use case), Docker-dependent sidecar is deployment burden. TEI solves model sharing on one machine, not cross-machine clustering. Still need Zenoh routing.
phase_2_5_adversarial.score_impact: TEI recommendation stands but with deployment complexity caveat

phase_2_5_adversarial.challenge: Just use candle, skip ONNX entirely
phase_2_5_adversarial.evidence: Nomic-embed-text-v1.5 uses RoPE + specific attention patterns that may not be fully optimized in candle. Migration requires numerical equivalence validation for 4 models. ort handles this via standardized ONNX format automatically.
phase_2_5_adversarial.score_impact: candle is Phase 3 not Phase 1; needs TEI to validate model coverage first

phase_2_5_adversarial.evidence: Zenoh provides transport, not consensus. No leader election, no service registry with health checks, no load balancing. Building service mesh from transport primitives is significant work.
phase_2_5_adversarial.score_impact: Zenoh is substrate not solution; need service-level abstractions on top

correctness.evidence_code: EmbedderTrait abstraction is clean seam for any swap
correctness.evidence_structural: HttpEmbedder proves external-service path works; no batch trait method

robustness.evidence_code: Circuit breaker wraps only MiniLM, not Nomic/NER
robustness.evidence_structural: Resilience pattern doesn't scale to networked inference (different timeout semantics)

security.evidence_code: SHA256 model verification, ORT_DYLIB_PATH pre-tokio, API key auth
security.evidence_structural: No mTLS/auth for Zenoh transport; cluster embedding traffic unauthenticated

performance.evidence_code: Single-threaded ONNX on ARM (intentional deadlock avoidance)
performance.evidence_structural: moka caches per-instance; cache miss amplification in cluster

scalability.evidence_code: Linear memory scaling per instance, no sharing mechanism
scalability.evidence_structural: Monolithic pattern breaks at cluster scale, Zenoh carries zero embedding traffic

maintainability.evidence_code: Clean module boundaries in embeddings/, handlers/, memory/
maintainability.evidence_structural: Consistent pattern across 4 models, env-var driven config

extensibility.evidence_structural: Zenoh feature gate pattern is right model for optional cluster features

data_integrity.evidence_structural: No merge strategy for concurrent writes from cluster peers

compliance.evidence_structural: No audit trail for cross-instance requests, no data residency controls

observability.evidence_code: Prometheus metrics, circuit breaker counters, cache hit rates
observability.evidence_structural: No distributed tracing for cross-instance embedding requests

testability.evidence_structural: No mock embedder in test harness, no cluster simulation

usability.evidence_structural: HttpEmbedder means zero-code-change path to shared serving

tract.verdict: VIABLE for ort replacement, not for clustering

recommended_architecture.phase_1: TEI sidecar for embedding serving. Route via existing HttpEmbedder (SHODH_EMBEDDING_API_URL). Zenoh for service discovery.
recommended_architecture.phase_2: Zenoh queryable/reply protocol for cross-node embedding request routing. Leader election for embedding node designation.
recommended_architecture.phase_3: candle migration to eliminate ONNX dependency entirely. TEI validates model support first.
